description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists in V8's Wasm-to-JS wrapper tier-up compilation. In `BuildWasmToJSWrapper()` (src/wasm/wrappers.cc), when a Wasm module imports a JavaScript function with an `expected_arity` of 0xfffa or more, the function constructs an `args` SmallVector of size `pushed_count + 4 + (has_dispatch_handle ? 1 : 0)`. When passed to the Turboshaft `CallOp` via `__ Call()`, the total input count (1 + args.size()) exceeds 65535, overflowing the `uint16_t input_count` field in the `Operation` base class (src/compiler/turboshaft/operations.h). This corrupts the `CallOp`'s stored `input_count` to a small or zero value. During the subsequent instruction selection phase in `InstructionSelectorT::InitializeCallBuffer()` (src/compiler/backend/instruction-selector.cc), the loop iterates over call arguments based on the correct large `CallDescriptor::InputCount()`, while `call.arguments()` returns a span backed by the corrupted (truncated) `input_count`. This mismatch causes the argument iterator to read `OpIndex` values from memory beyond the call operation's input array. When `GetVirtualRegister()` processes these out-of-bounds `OpIndex` values, their computed IDs exceed the size of `virtual_registers_`, resulting in an out-of-bounds heap memory read (and potential write) — a full out-of-V8-sandbox memory corruption. The vulnerability is triggered during Wasm-to-JS wrapper tier-up (`Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper`) for an imported JS function whose `length` property reports 0xfffa or more parameters.

fixes.evidence_text: merges approved for [https://crrev.com/c/6249299](<https://crrev.com/c/6249299>), please merge to the respective V8 branches ASAP, by 10am PT tomorrow (Friday) so this fix can be included in next week's updates.
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6249299 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
